forecasts.reasoning: Beto O'Rourke is visibly testing the waters for a 2026 Senate run, holding town halls in Texas (e.g., Denton in April 2025, Dallas on May 19, 2025) and explicitly stating he "will" run if Texans want him to. However, the resolution criteria require him to announce before July 1, 2025, which gives a narrow ~5-week window from the May 22 knowledge cutoff. While a June announcement is plausible to capture momentum or an end-of-quarter FEC boost, candidates often extend "exploratory" phases through the summer or wait until the fall (as Beto did when he launched his 2022 Gubernatorial bid in November 2021). Furthermore, he can legally continue touring and mobilizing via his "Powered by People" PAC without formally launching a Senate committee. Given the tight time constraint and the possibility that he delays or ultimately decides against a fourth consecutive major run, the probability of a formal announcement *before* July 1 is estimated at roughly 1 in 3.

Following the Pahalgam terror attack in April 2025, India launched 'Operation Sindoor' on May 7, executing kinetic strikes in Pakistan proper (Punjab province). This led to a brief, intense escalation. However, by May 10-11, both sides agreed to a ceasefire following high-level US intervention (including statements from President Trump). While there was some subsequent border friction and Indian leaders continue to use aggressive rhetoric for deterrence ('not over yet', 'just a trailer'), significant de-escalation steps have occurred, such as Pakistan returning a captured BSF jawan on May 14, and India's External Affairs Minister confirming on May 22 that the Pakistani Army initiated the de-escalation. This cycle mirrors the 2019 Pulwama/Balakot crisis. Given that direct strikes into Pakistan proper are massive escalatory steps, they are highly unlikely to be repeated in June without another major terror provocation. With the ceasefire holding and international pressure maintained, the risk has significantly subsided.

The base rate for a pilot reporting a UFO to a US-based English-language database (NUFORC) from a country in Africa is extremely low. Narrowing this down to a single specific month (June 2025) makes the baseline probability infinitesimal. I searched for any current UFO 'flaps', organized reporting campaigns by African pilot associations, or recent institutional changes at NUFORC that could increase these numbers, but found nothing. Because this is a highly specific conjunction of factors happening in a specific 30-day window, the probability is conservatively at the lowest allowable threshold.

On May 9, 2025, just two weeks before the knowledge cutoff, Rippling announced a $450 million private funding round at a $16.8 billion valuation, accompanied by a $200 million tender offer for employee liquidity. In an interview about the primary raise, CEO Parker Conrad explicitly stated that the company isn't planning for an IPO in the near future and that an IPO is 'not imminent.' It is practically unheard of for a company to complete a major private funding round with an employee tender offer (which serves as a substitute for IPO liquidity) and then announce an IPO within the subsequent six or seven weeks. Given the unequivocal recent statements ruling out an IPO, the probability of an announcement before July 1, 2025, is exceedingly low.

The government of Mali, led by a military junta under Gen Assimi Goïta, issued a presidential decree on May 13, 2025, completely dissolving all political parties and political organizations. This structural move followed "national consultations" in April where it was recommended that Goïta's rule be extended for another five years (until 2030). Given that this decree was just formalized weeks before the July 1, 2025, resolution date, the timeline for a reversal is extremely short. Furthermore, the junta has deeply entrenched itself against external pressure by withdrawing from the regional bloc ECOWAS, which previously applied sanctions to force democratic transitions. With political opposition leaders being arrested and civil space systematically closed, there is virtually no domestic or international mechanism likely to force a full or partial reversal in the next ~45 days. The probability is set to the floor value of 0.02.

Databricks has made no public announcement or S-1 filing as of May 22, 2025. In late 2024/January 2025, they raised a historic $10 billion funding round, providing ample liquidity for employees and substantially reducing internal pressure to go public. Financial media in early 2025 (e.g., Bloomberg in March 2025) grouped Databricks with "perennially private" firms keeping their IPOs "on ice." With only ~5 weeks remaining between the knowledge cutoff and the July 1, 2025 resolution date, and absolutely no recent leaks about hiring underwriters or confidentially filing an S-1, the likelihood of a formal announcement or public S-1 filing in this very narrow window is extremely low.

The question asks if Donald Trump or Elon Musk will tweet 'diversity visa' before either April 1 or July 1, 2025. Donald Trump explicitly tweeted the phrase "Diversity Visa Lottery Program" on November 1, 2017. The operational resolution criteria dictates checking a specific Twitter search link (that queries for "diversity visa" from either account) on/after July 1, 2025. Because the criteria simply states "If a tweet... containing the phrase is seen by a Metaculus Admin at that time, this question resolves as Yes", it is highly likely it resolves Yes based purely on Trump's 2017 tweet appearing in the results. Even if admins interpret the title ("Will [they] tweet") to require a *new* tweet, Elon Musk has been actively engaging with accounts (like fentasyl) who talk about this, and border/immigration is a massive talking point for both. Thus, there is a strong base probability of a new tweet, plus an extremely high chance of early resolution via Trump's 2017 tweet satisfying the literal criteria. I estimate 83% to account for both the strong technical yes probability and the possibility they genuinely tweet about it soon, buffered by a small risk of annulment if admins heavily scrutinize the 'verb' tense in the title versus the specific search criteria.
